---
title: "Untitled"
format: html
editor: visual
---

## Quarto

Quarto enables you to weave together content and executable code into a finished document. To learn more about Quarto see <https://quarto.org>.

## Running Code

When you click the **Render** button a document will be generated that includes both content and the output of embedded code. You can embed code like this:

```{r}
1 + 1
```

You can add options to executable code like this

```{r}
#| echo: false
2 * 2
```

The `echo: false` option disables the printing of code (only output is displayed).

```{r}
library(OneR)

```

```{r}
# URL of the Mushroom dataset
url <- "https://archive.ics.uci.edu/ml/machine-learning-databases/mushroom/agaricus-lepiota.data"

# Download the file (optional if you've already downloaded it manually)
download.file(url, destfile = "mushroom.csv")

# Read the dataset into R
# Assuming the dataset doesn't have a header row and columns need to be named manually
mushroom_data <- read.csv("mushroom.csv", header = FALSE)

# Optionally, add column names if the dataset doesn't have any
colnames(mushroom_data) <- c("column1", "column2", "column3", ...) # replace with actual column names

```

```{r}
# URL of the Mushroom dataset
url <- "https://archive.ics.uci.edu/ml/machine-learning-databases/mushroom/agaricus-lepiota.data"

# Download the file (optional if you've already downloaded it manually)
download.file(url, destfile = "mushroom.csv")

# Read the dataset into R
# Assuming the dataset doesn't have a header row, you need to add column names
mushroom_data <- read.csv("mushroom.csv", header = FALSE)

# Define the column names as per the dataset's description
column_names <- c("class", "cap_shape", "cap_surface", "cap_color", "bruises", "odor",
                  "gill_attachment", "gill_spacing", "gill_size", "gill_color",
                  "stalk_shape", "stalk_root", "stalk_surface_above_ring", 
                  "stalk_surface_below_ring", "stalk_color_above_ring", 
                  "stalk_color_below_ring", "veil_type", "veil_color", "ring_number", 
                  "ring_type", "spore_print_color", "population", "habitat")

# Assign the column names to the dataset
colnames(mushroom_data) <- column_names

```

```{r}
# Read the dataset into R
mushroom_data <- read.csv("mushroom.csv", header = FALSE)

# Define the column names as per the dataset's documentation
column_names <- c("class", "cap_shape", "cap_surface", "cap_color", "bruises", "odor",
                  "gill_attachment", "gill_spacing", "gill_size", "gill_color",
                  "stalk_shape", "stalk_root", "stalk_surface_above_ring", 
                  "stalk_surface_below_ring", "stalk_color_above_ring", 
                  "stalk_color_below_ring", "veil_type", "veil_color", "ring_number", 
                  "ring_type", "spore_print_color", "population", "habitat")

# Assign the column names to the dataset
colnames(mushroom_data) <- column_names

```

```{r}
table(mushrooms$cap_shape, mushrooms$type)
```

```{r}
table(mushroom_data$cap_shape, mushroom_data$class)

```

```{r}
library(OneR)  # Ensure the OneR package is loaded

mush_1R <- OneR(class ~ ., data = mushroom_data, verbose = TRUE)

```

```{r}
head(iris)
```

```{r}
data <- optbin(iris)
head(data)
```

```{r}
model <- OneR(data, verbose = TRUE)
```

```{r}
summary(model)
```

```{r}
plot(model)
```

```{r}
prediction <- predict(model, data)

eval_model(prediction, data)
```

```{r}
data(breastcancer)
data <- breastcancer
```

```{r}
set.seed(12) # for reproducibility
random <- sample(1:nrow(data), 0.8 * nrow(data))
data_train <- optbin(data[random, ], method = "infogain")
```

```{r}
data_test <- data[-random, ]

```

```{r}
model_train <- OneR(data_train, verbose = TRUE)

```

```{r}
summary(model_train)

```

```{r}
plot(model_train)

```

```{r}
prediction <- predict(model_train, data_test)

```

```{r}
eval_model(prediction, data_test)

```

```{r}
data <- iris
str(data)
```

```{r}
str(bin(data))

```

```{r}
str(bin(data, nbins = 3, labels = c("small", "medium", "large")))

```

```{r}
set.seed(1); table(bin(rnorm(900), nbins = 3))

```

```{r}
set.seed(1); table(bin(rnorm(900), nbins = 3, method = "content"))

```

```{r}
intervals <- paste(levels(bin(faithful$waiting, nbins = 2, method = "cluster")), collapse = " ")
hist(faithful$waiting, main = paste("Intervals:", intervals))
abline(v = c(42.9, 67.5, 96.1), col = "blue")
```

```{r}
bin(c(1:10, NA), nbins = 2, na.omit = FALSE) # adds new level "NA"

```

```{r}
bin(c(1:10, NA), nbins = 2)

```

```{r}
df <- data.frame(numeric = c(1:26), alphabet = letters)
str(df)
```

```{r}
str(maxlevels(df))

```

```{r}
model <- OneR(iris)
predict(model, data.frame(Petal.Width = seq(0, 3, 0.5)))
```

```{r}
predict(model, data.frame(Petal.Width = seq(0, 3, 0.5)), type = "prob")

```

```{r}
help(package = OneR)

```
